project.title: DARPin_study -  Systemic analysis of tyrosine kinase signaling reveals a common adaptive response program in a HER2-positive breast cancer
project.projectDescription: Studying signaling networks downstream of HER2 inhibition
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: immunoprecipitation of tyrosine phosphoryation followed by IMAC and LCMS analysis
project.dataProcessingProtocol: MASCOT analysis in proteome discoverer using trypsin and dynamic modifications

references.referenceLine: Schwill M, Tamaskovic R, Gajadhar AS, Kast F, White FM, Plückthun A. Systemic analysis of tyrosine kinase signaling reveals a common adaptive response program in a HER2-positive breast cancer. Sci Signal. 2019 12(565)

Characteristics[MaterialType]: human mammary carcinoma cell lines BT474, SKBR3, HCC1419, MDA-MB453, UACC893 and MDA-MB361
Characteristics[Specimen]: Cell pellets were lysed in 8 M urea supplemented with phosphatase inhibitors on ice
Characteristics[CleavageAgent]: digested with trypsin overnight at room temperature
Characteristics[ReductionReagent]: reduced with 1 M DTT in ammonium acetate at pH 8.9
Characteristics[AlkylationReagent]: 55 mM iodoacetamide was added to quench reformation of disulfide bridges.
Comment[EnrichmentMethod]: added to 60 μL protein G agarose beads, which were loaded with anti-phosphotyrosine antibodies (12 μg 4G10 (Millipore), 12 μg PY-100 (Cell Signaling Technologies), and 12 μg PT-66 (Sigma)) overnight at 4 °C
Comment[Instrument]: Thermo Q Exactive Hybrid Quadrupole-Orbitrap mass spectrometer
Comment[Separation]: self-packed analytical capillary column [50 μm ID × 12 cm packed with 5 μm C18 beads (YMC gel, ODS-AQ, 12 nm, S-5 μm, AQ12S05)] with an integrated electrospray tip (~1 μm orifice)
Comment[IonizationType]: ionized with a spray voltage of 2 kV
